subagent_reports.topic: Discovery opportunity scout — dashboard login-start, stable URL, low CPU/API, GitHub auth guidance

sources.label: bin/lib/dashboard/server.ts createDashboardHandler default stateBuilder
sources.summary: Per-request default stateBuilder calls buildDashboardState(config), which constructs a fresh createGitHubDashboardClient each request. The ETag cache Map lives inside that client, so it is discarded on every /api/state poll — no server-side response cache/TTL.

sources.label: bin/lib/dashboard/github.ts readRepository fan-out
sources.summary: Each repo read fans out to pulls + actions/runs + deployments, then one statuses call PER deployment (up to 30). Every poll re-fetches all endpoints for all projects with no warm ETag cache, directly stressing the low-CPU/API constraint.

sources.label: bin/lib/dashboard/github.ts createEnvTokenProvider
sources.summary: Token provider only reads GITHUB_TOKEN env; absence yields GITHUB_AUTH_UNAVAILABLE degraded source. Environment has gh CLI authenticated but no GITHUB_TOKEN, so dashboard would degrade despite available auth.

sources.label: gh auth token (GitHub CLI manual)
sources.summary: `gh auth token` outputs the active account auth token for scripts — canonical, boring fallback to bridge gh-authenticated environments without exporting GITHUB_TOKEN.

sources.label: systemd user service WantedBy=default.target (man systemd.special / Unix StackExchange)
sources.summary: For non-GUI user services, WantedBy=default.target (with loginctl enable-linger for boot-time) is the canonical login-start mechanism. There is no user multi-user.target. Matches existing temporal-dev.service / vision.service repo precedent (Type=simple, Restart=on-failure, WantedBy=default.target).

sources.label: bin/adv dashboard dispatcher requires --config
sources.summary: dashboard subcommand hard-requires --config absolute path and per-project github owner/repo; no default/generated config path exists for a login-started service to consume.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: The proposal's stated constraints (login-start, stable URL, low CPU/API, GitHub-auth guidance) map cleanly onto the existing dashboard with NO new architecture. The dominant risk is the existing per-request GitHub fan-out with a discarded ETag cache: a login-started background service that is polled by one or more open browser tabs every 30-60s will multiply API calls and conflict with the low-CPU/API constraint. The boring, proven fixes are: (1) a single long-lived stateBuilder closure that reuses one GitHubDashboardClient (preserving its ETag cache) plus a short server-side TTL so concurrent tabs share one upstream fetch; (2) a `gh auth token` fallback in the token provider to use the already-available gh auth; (3) a `WantedBy=default.target` user systemd unit mirroring the repo's existing temporal-dev/vision services; (4) a default/generated config for the two PokeEdge repos so the service has something to serve at login. All four are in-scope and tie to stated constraints. No candidate proposes hosted service, mutation, analytics, or browser auto-open.

validation.notes: No release/security/contract blockers. Caution flag is for the cache-reuse candidate: it is a behavior change to the server lifecycle (long-lived client + TTL) that must preserve per-request degraded-source isolation already present in readAdvProjectSafely/readGitHubProjectSafely. The two CLARIFY warnings on the change (auth model, error handling) are partially answered here: auth model = read-only gh token via `gh auth token`, no auth material persisted (sanitizeDashboardState already redacts); error handling = existing per-source degraded model plus a clear GitHub setup prompt. Token sanitization path (config.ts sanitizeString/isSecretKey) already covers the must-not-expose-secrets constraint.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt now into the agreement: (A) reuse a single GitHubDashboardClient across requests + add a short server-side state TTL/in-flight de-dupe so the ETag cache survives and concurrent browser tabs share one upstream read — this is the primary lever for the low-CPU/API constraint. (B) Extend the token provider with a `gh auth token` fallback when GITHUB_TOKEN is unset, and reshape GITHUB_AUTH_UNAVAILABLE into actionable setup guidance (run `gh auth login`). (C) Ship a WantedBy=default.target user systemd unit modeled on existing temporal-dev.service/vision.service (Type=simple, Restart=on-failure) for login-start + stable http://127.0.0.1:8765 URL. (D) Provide a default/generated config for ~/dev/pokeedge and ~/dev/pokeedge-web so --config is satisfied at login. Surface to user: confirm fixed port 8765 reservation and exact stable URL, and confirm `gh auth token` invocation is acceptable (it shells out to gh). Defer: missing-project tolerance polish beyond existing per-source degradation.
follow_ups: Confirm with user: reserve port 8765 and stable URL http://127.0.0.1:8765 as the daily URL, including collision behavior when port is taken.
Confirm with user: token provider may shell out to `gh auth token` (subprocess) vs env-only — affects the auth model CLARIFY warning.
Design phase: decide default-config location (user-level XDG vs Advance-owned generated file) so observed PokeEdge repos do not require repo-local config files.
Verify low-CPU/API claim empirically in design/acceptance: measure GitHub API calls per refresh interval with N open tabs before vs after client/TTL reuse.

subagent_reports.topic: Design leverage scout — dashboard autostart: port-collision loudness, restart-loop guard, linger self-enable, cache lifecycle, gh-token fallback

sources.label: bin/adv runDashboard Bun.serve — no try/catch, no reusePort
sources.summary: runDashboard calls bun.serve({hostname,port,fetch}) with NO try/catch and NO reusePort:false. On port collision it relies entirely on Bun's default listen behavior — which historically did not throw (SO_REUSEPORT). AC4/C4 (fail loudly, no silent fallback) is not structurally guaranteed today.

sources.label: Bun GitHub #1443 / #7207 — Bun.serve silent port reuse
sources.summary: Bun.serve did not throw on port-in-use because reusePort/SO_REUSEPORT was the implicit default; later made explicit via reusePort:true. Two servers could bind the same port silently. Design must verify current Bun throws EADDRINUSE on a real bound port and explicitly set reusePort:false, then map the error to a distinct non-restart exit code.

sources.label: systemd.service(5) RestartPreventExitStatus + StartLimitIntervalSec/Burst
sources.summary: RestartPreventExitStatus= is the canonical mechanism to stop auto-restart for a specific fatal exit code regardless of Restart=. StartLimitIntervalSec/StartLimitBurst is the rate-limit backstop. Design point 4 is by-the-book; pairing both is defense-in-depth against a restart storm on persistent port collision.

sources.label: loginctl(1) enable-linger — no root for self
sources.summary: enable-linger with no USER arg targets the caller's own user and spawns a user manager at boot kept across logouts — does not require root for self. Installer/doctor CAN self-enable linger for the current user without sudo, enabling a one-shot AC2 remediation rather than emit-only guidance.

sources.label: buildDashboardState per-request client (discovery scout, re-confirmed)
sources.summary: createDashboardHandler default stateBuilder calls buildDashboardState(config) per /api/state request; buildDashboardState constructs a fresh createGitHubDashboardClient each call, discarding its in-client ETag cache. No server-side TTL or in-flight de-dupe exists. The stateBuilder injection seam already exists, so a long-lived client + TTL is a low-risk closure swap in runDashboard, not an architecture change.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Draft design maps cleanly onto existing code with no new architecture; the dominant value is in two under-specified correctness risks, not the install scaffolding. (1) PORT-COLLISION LOUDNESS (AC4/C4): runDashboard's bun.serve has no error handling and Bun historically swallowed port-in-use via SO_REUSEPORT — fail-loud is NOT structurally guaranteed. The design must (a) set reusePort:false, (b) wrap serve in try/catch, (c) emit a clear diagnostic, (d) exit with a distinct code wired to RestartPreventExitStatus. These are coupled; doing the systemd RestartPreventExitStatus (point 4) WITHOUT the bun.serve guard leaves the loud-failure path unproven. (2) CACHE LIFECYCLE (AC7/C6/SC4): the per-request fresh GitHub client discards the ETag cache every poll; the existing stateBuilder injection seam makes a long-lived-client + short TTL + in-flight de-dupe a low-risk closure swap. These two are the highest-leverage, contract-tied items. The install/doctor/systemd/config scaffolding (points 1-3,6-8) is boring and correct; the one upgrade is that linger self-enable needs no root, so AC2 remediation can be one-shot rather than emit-only. Add StartLimitIntervalSec/Burst as a restart-storm backstop beyond RestartPreventExitStatus.

validation.notes: No release/security/contract blockers. Caution: AC4 (fail loudly on port collision) is currently unproven in code — runDashboard's bun.serve lacks try/catch and reusePort:false, and Bun's historical default was silent reuse. Treat the bun.serve collision guard + distinct exit code as a required design element coupled to the systemd RestartPreventExitStatus, with a TDD red test that binds 8765 first and asserts a non-zero distinct exit + diagnostic. AC7 cache-coalescing requires preserving the existing per-source degraded isolation in readAdvProjectSafely/readGitHubProjectSafely when moving to a long-lived client. No conflict with constraints/avoidances (still local-only, read-only, no browser auto-open).

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt now (contract-tied, low risk): (A) In runDashboard set reusePort:false, wrap bun.serve in try/catch, emit a clear collision diagnostic, and exit with a distinct dedicated code; wire that code into the systemd unit's RestartPreventExitStatus and add StartLimitIntervalSec/StartLimitBurst as a backstop — closes AC4/C4 and prevents a restart storm. Add a TDD red test that pre-binds 8765 and asserts the distinct non-zero exit. (B) Make runDashboard inject a long-lived stateBuilder that reuses ONE createGitHubDashboardClient (preserving its ETag cache) plus a short server-side TTL (= refresh_seconds) and in-flight /api/state promise de-dupe via the existing stateBuilder seam — closes AC7/SC4/C6 with no architecture change. Design-around: (C) doctor/install may self-enable linger via `loginctl enable-linger` (no root for caller) as a one-shot AC2 remediation, falling back to emit-only guidance if it fails. Surface to user (medium): confirm a fixed dedicated exit code value for port collision and whether doctor should auto-run enable-linger vs only print it. Follow-up: empirically measure GitHub API calls per refresh with N tabs before/after the cache change during acceptance.
follow_ups: TASK_SCOPE/IN_SCOPE/OUT_OF_SCOPE/DONE_WHEN/STOP_WHEN/VERIFICATION anchors were not all present in the packet; proceeded with prompt scope (design-mode leverage scouting) per skill routing.
Design: pick the concrete distinct exit code for port collision and document it in both bin/adv and the systemd unit RestartPreventExitStatus.
Acceptance: add a test that binds 8765 first, then starts the dashboard, asserting a distinct non-zero exit and a no-fallback-port diagnostic (AC4).
Acceptance: measure GitHub API request count per refresh interval with multiple open tabs before vs after the long-lived-client + TTL change (AC7/SC4).

subagent_reports.topic: Design validation: dashboard autostart (systemd user service, port-collision hardening, runtime cache, gh auth fallback)

sources.label: systemd.service(5) — RestartPreventExitStatus / Restart=on-failure
sources.summary: RestartPreventExitStatus prevents auto-restart for a specific exit code regardless of Restart=; Restart=on-failure is the recommended choice for long-running services. Validates D2 collision exit-code design.

sources.label: systemd.unit(5) — StartLimitIntervalSec/StartLimitBurst
sources.summary: Start rate limiting is particularly useful in conjunction with Restart=; units exceeding burst within interval are not restarted. Validates D2/D5 restart-storm backstop.

sources.label: systemd#2690 + serverfault — user-service WantedBy=default.target + linger
sources.summary: systemd maintainers: no multi-user.target in --user mode; user services must be WantedBy=default.target. loginctl enable-linger required to run after logout / before first login. Validates D1/D4.

sources.label: GitHub CLI manual — gh auth token
sources.summary: gh auth token prints the auth token for the active account/host. Canonical local-auth fallback. Validates D6.

sources.label: Bun PR #30364 + Bun.serve reference
sources.summary: Bun.serve throws on bind failure with code EADDRINUSE (now with non-zero errno). reusePort (SO_REUSEPORT) is opt-in, Linux-only, default off. Validates D2 try/catch + no-silent-fallback.

sources.label: Repo evidence — server.ts / github.ts / bin/adv
sources.summary: buildDashboardState creates a fresh GitHub client (with per-client ETag cache Map) on every /api/state request, confirming D5 cache-discard rationale. tokenProvider is a pluggable seam and GITHUB_TOKEN handling already exists, confirming D6 is a clean extension. bin/adv Bun.serve has no try/catch, confirming D2 hardening is a real gap. Repo already uses gh subprocess (roadmap.ts, git-finalize.ts) + gh auth status (gh-cli.ts), so D6 follows existing convention.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is correct, boring, and well-grounded. All four load-bearing technical decisions match canonical references: (D1/D4) user systemd service with WantedBy=default.target + loginctl enable-linger is the by-the-book mechanism for autostart-after-logout; (D2) Bun.serve throws catchable EADDRINUSE and RestartPreventExitStatus + StartLimit* is the canonical restart-storm backstop; (D5) cache/coalescing addresses a verified per-request client+ETag-discard inefficiency in server.ts; (D6) gh auth token is the canonical fallback and follows existing repo gh-subprocess convention. Design is scoped outside .adv/specs/ spec-law (bin/adv tooling), so no spec conflict is possible. Test seams (fakeable systemctl/loginctl adapters, deterministic unit-content tests, pre-bound-port regression) are appropriate and avoid host mutation.

validation.notes: VALIDATED. No blockers. Minor non-blocking observations: (1) systemd docs suggest Type=exec over Type=simple for more accurate start-failure reporting (e.g. missing executable) — optional refinement, Type=simple is acceptable. (2) reusePort is Linux-only and already defaults to false; explicitly setting reusePort:false is belt-and-suspenders, correctly hedged by 'where supported'. (3) loginctl enable-linger for the caller's own session can work without sudo via no-arg form, but may be policy-blocked; design's 'attempt then report exact remediation' correctly handles this. (4) Confirm the distinct port-collision exit code does not collide with the existing exit-code 2 used for config/server errors (bin/adv) and exit-code 3 documented for dashboard config errors — pick a dedicated code (e.g. RestartPreventExitStatus target) and keep it disjoint.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Proceed to planning. Design is VALIDATED against agreement objectives, AC1-AC9, and external canonical docs. During planning, (a) reserve a dedicated, disjoint exit code for port collision and wire it identically into both the Bun catch path and RestartPreventExitStatus; (b) consider Type=exec for the unit; (c) ensure DDC6 adapter seams cover loginctl enable-linger permission-denied so AC2 remediation path is unit-tested without host mutation.
follow_ups: Planning: confirm port-collision exit code is disjoint from existing bin/adv exit codes 2/3 and matches RestartPreventExitStatus.
Planning: evaluate Type=exec vs Type=simple for adv-dashboard-pokeedge.service per systemd start-failure-reporting guidance.

Do not turn the dashboard into generic BI, trend analytics, or historical reporting.
Do not auto-open a browser tab at login.

Do not store or display GitHub tokens or secrets.
Do not use high-frequency polling that creates noticeable CPU load or API churn.
Hosted multi-user dashboard or external backend.
Dashboard write/mutation controls.
BI/trend analytics, historical reporting, or portfolio reporting.
Auto-opening a browser tab at login.
Provider-specific deployment API expansion.

documents.proposal:
# Improve dashboard autostart

## Why

The local dashboard currently behaves like a manual development tool: it must be started explicitly and configured ad hoc before it is useful. That makes it easy to forget, unavailable when the user needs it, and less effective as a day-to-day operator surface for PokeEdge work.

The dashboard should become a lightweight, login-started, read-only local service with a stable URL, focused on the two PokeEdge projects the user actively wants to watch.

## What Changes

- Make the local dashboard reliably available after machine login without requiring manual startup.
- Provide a stable local URL for the dashboard.
- Configure the dashboard to observe:
  - `~/dev/pokeedge`
  - `~/dev/pokeedge-web`
- Keep background behavior lightweight, especially in CPU usage and GitHub/API usage.
- Improve the GitHub-auth-unavailable experience from noisy degraded state to clear setup guidance.
- Preserve dashboard v1 as local-only and read-only.

## User Outcomes

- User can open a known local URL after login and see the PokeEdge dashboard without first running a command.
- User can trust that the dashboard is present in the background while working, without noticeable CPU/API overhead.
- User can see both configured PokeEdge projects from one dashboard view.
- User gets clear GitHub setup guidance when auth/API access is unavailable.
- User does not see mutation controls or generic analytics/trend reporting.

## Affected Code

- Local dashboard CLI/runtime entrypoint.
- Dashboard configuration loading/defaulting.
- Dashboard documentation and setup guidance.
- GitHub-auth unavailable/degraded presentation.
- Possible local startup integration surface, pending discovery.

## Related Repositories

Primary implementation repo:
- `advance` (`/home/jon/dev/advance`)

Configured observed repos:
- `~/dev/pokeedge`
- `~/dev/pokeedge-web`

The observed repos are dashboard inputs, not necessarily implementation targets. Discovery verified both directories exist, are ADV-enabled, and have GitHub remotes:
- `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge` → `Sharper-Flow/PokeEdge`
- `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge-web` → `Sharper-Flow/PokeEdge-Web`

## Constraints

- Must remain local-only.
- Must remain read-only.
- Must run in the background after login and not open a browser automatically.
- Must provide a stable local URL.
- Must prioritize low CPU/API usage over aggressive refresh.
- Must preserve clear behavior when GitHub auth/API is unavailable.
- Must not expose secrets or auth material in config, logs, or browser output.

## Impact

This makes the dashboard useful as a persistent operator surface rather than a manual preview tool. It reduces startup friction and makes PokeEdge project state easier to check routinely.

## Context

Prior dashboard work delivered a read-only local dashboard with explicit project config, structural correlation, per-source degradation, and a browser UI. Follow-up usage clarified that the next valuable improvement is not more data or write actions, but making the dashboard automatically available for the specific PokeEdge projects with low overhead.

## Scope

### In Scope

- Login-start/background availability for the local dashboard.
- Stable local URL behavior.
- Default or generated configuration for `~/dev/pokeedge` and `~/dev/pokeedge-web`.
- Low CPU/API operation model.
- GitHub setup guidance when authentication/API access is unavailable.
- Documentation for setup, operation, and troubleshooting.

### Out of Scope

- Hosted dashboard service.
- Dashboard write actions or mutation controls.
- Generic BI dashboard, trend analytics, or historical reporting.
- Auto-opening a browser tab at login.
- Provider-specific deployment API expansion.

### Must Not

- Must not mutate ADV state, GitHub state, deployment state, or repository state.
- Must not run high-frequency polling that creates noticeable CPU load or API churn.
- Must not require the dashboard to be opened manually before the background service exists.
- Must not store or display GitHub tokens or secrets.
- Must not turn this into a general-purpose hosted dashboard product.

## Discovery Agenda

### Codebase unknowns

- Current dashboard command/config entrypoints and where persistent defaults should live.
- Existing repo conventions for user-level config, background processes, wrappers, and generated files.
- Whether the dashboard can reuse existing config parsing as-is or needs a separate user-level default config path.

### Ecosystem unknowns

- Best login-start mechanism for this environment: user systemd service, shell/session hook, OpenCode-adjacent wrapper, or another lightweight mechanism.
- How to make startup reliable without adding daemon complexity or high idle overhead.

### Domain unknowns

- Preferred fixed port and collision behavior.
- Exact static URL to reserve for daily use.
- How explicit the GitHub auth setup prompt should be and whether it should link to commands/docs.

### Integration unknowns

- Whether `~/dev/pokeedge` and `~/dev/pokeedge-web` are both ADV-enabled and how their project IDs/state should be resolved.
- Whether any active dashboard/autostart process already exists outside this repository.
- How startup should behave if one configured project is missing or temporarily unavailable.

## Discovery Findings

### Discovery Checklist

| Step | Result | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| Skill Discovery | PASS | No pending-review skills found. No exact local-autostart skill matched; existing skills were tangential. |
| Prior Research Extension | PASS | Cited repo-improve, adv-run-test, slop-scan prep docs plus archived `addLocalDashboard`. |
| Conflict & Related-Work Scan | PASS | No conflicting active change found; own validation passed with expected proposal-stage warnings. |
| Edge Case Investigation | PASS | Edge cases documented for autostart, config, auth, API usage, and missing projects. |
| Design Question Depth | PASS | Open questions include trust model, blast radius, alternatives, and recommendations. |
| Draft Spec Delta Shapes | PASS | Draft `rq-localDashboard*` shapes captured; new local-dashboard spec likely needed. |
| P25 Related-Pattern Scan | PASS | Similar patterns found in `bin/adv`, dashboard modules, user services, and prep docs. |
| LBP Check | PASS | User systemd service + lingering, generated config, gh-token fallback, and TTL cache are the likely LBP. |
| Completeness Verification | PASS | Problem and solution scope cover lifecycle, config, auth, URL, low API, and failure behavior. |

### Skills Considered

- `adv-frontend-review`: tangential; visual surface exists, but this change is lifecycle/autostart first.
- `customize-opencode`: not loaded; this is Advance product code, not OpenCode config customization.
- `adv-ci-release`: tangential; no CI/CD release pattern decision.
- `adv-worktree`: not applicable; no worktree lifecycle change in discovery.
- No core skill for local login services/autostart was found. Gap recorded; no skill created because the evidence needed here is narrow and current discovery already cites local user-service precedents plus systemd/XDG sources.

### Extends

- Archived `addLocalDashboard`: delivered `adv dashboard`, static UI, explicit config, GET-only routes, 30–60s refresh, per-source degradation, and read-only behavior. New finding: autostart requires service lifecycle, default config, cache reuse, and setup prompt behavior beyond the archived dashboard implementation.
- `docs/repo-improve-prep.md`: warns hot status views can do too much work (`adv_status view:"summary"` builds full payload; lines 35–46). New finding: dashboard autostart must avoid reproducing that pattern by caching/de-duping GitHub reads and not doing work when no browser/state request occurs.
- `docs/adv-run-test-prep.md`: notes existing user services `temporal-dev.service` and `vision.service` (lines 163–169). New finding: this repo already uses user-level systemd services with `WantedBy=default.target`, `Restart=on-failure`, and journal logging, providing a local precedent.
- `docs/adv-slop-scan-prep.md`: rejects enterprise dashboards for local command determinism (lines 100–126). New finding: this dashboard should remain local operator status, not expand into BI/trend analytics.

### Conflict Scan

- `adv_change_list` active scan found `improveDashboardAutostart` and unrelated active changes (`reduceAdvPromptSlop`, `addCiWaitAgent`, `fixWedgedWorktreeCleanup`, etc.); no direct active autostart/dashboard duplicate.
- `adv_change_validate improveDashboardAutostart` passed. Warnings `NO_TASKS` and `NO_DELTAS` are expected before prep/spec delta decisions.
- `adv_agenda_list` is large; no obvious dashboard-autostart duplicate in returned high-signal subset. Related general issues: status overhead and user-service observations are already captured through prep docs.
- Archived `addLocalDashboard` is direct prior work and informs constraints: keep local, read-only, explicit project config, source degradation, and 30–60s refresh.

### Current State

- `bin/adv dashboard` currently hard-requires `--config`; missing config exits with `dashboard: --config is required` (`bin/adv:225-229`).
- Dashboard default host is `127.0.0.1`, default port is `8765`, and non-loopback hosts require explicit opt-in (`server.ts:268-282`).
- Dashboard exposes only `GET /` and `GET /api/state`; mutation methods return 405 (`server.ts:236-258`).
- Config parser requires schema version 1, 30–60s `refresh_seconds`, absolute project paths, and GitHub owner/repo (`config.ts:81-133`).
- GitHub client fetches pulls, workflow runs, deployments, and per-deployment status (`github.ts:85-117`).
- GitHub token provider currently checks only `GITHUB_TOKEN` (`github.ts:49-54`), but local `gh auth status` is authenticated.
- `buildDashboardState` creates a fresh GitHub client per state build (`server.ts:48-60`), so ETag cache is not reused across `/api/state` polls.
- Local systemd user manager is running; existing user services include `temporal-dev.service` and `vision.service` with `Type=simple`, `Restart=on-failure`, `WantedBy=default.target`.
- `loginctl show-user $USER --property=Linger` returned `Linger=no`, so always-running after logout requires enabling or documenting lingering.
- `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge` and `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge-web` both contain `project.json`, are git repos, and have GitHub remotes under `Sharper-Flow`.

### Edge Cases

Autostart/lifecycle:
- User manager unavailable or `systemctl --user` fails on a minimal/non-systemd environment.
- Linger is disabled, so service starts after login but stops after logout unless setup enables or prompts for lingering.
- Dashboard executable path changes after repo move or local deploy; service must fail clearly rather than silently running stale code.

Stable URL/port:
- Port `8765` is occupied by another process at login.
- Port `8765` is occupied by an existing healthy dashboard instance.
- Service restart loops if collision is treated as retryable without clear diagnostics.

Config/projects:
- One of `~/dev/pokeedge` or `~/dev/pokeedge-web` is missing or moved.
- Repo exists but is not a git repo or ADV project resolution fails.
- GitHub owner/repo config drifts from remote URL.

GitHub auth/API:
- `GITHUB_TOKEN` is absent but `gh auth token` works.
- Neither env token nor `gh auth token` works.
- Rate limit or secondary throttle is returned for one project but not the other.
- Token or auth error text could leak sensitive values if not sanitized.

Low CPU/API:
- Multiple browser tabs poll `/api/state` concurrently.
- Refresh interval causes repeated full GitHub fanout for two repos.
- Deployments endpoint with up to 30 deployments causes up to 30 status calls per repo per refresh.

### Open Design Questions

1. **Autostart mechanism** — Trust model: agent. Blast radius: wrong choice makes service unreliable or too invasive. Alternatives: user systemd service, XDG autostart desktop file, shell/session hook, OpenCode wrapper. Recommendation: user systemd service, because current environment supports it, existing repo-adjacent services use it, and it supports background supervision/logs.
2. **Persistence after logout** — Trust model: user + agent. Blast radius: without lingering, dashboard is not always running as user requested; with lingering, setup may require operator action. Alternatives: login-only, enable lingering, or document optional lingering. User resolved: should always run even logged out, so design should include linger detection/setup guidance.
3. **Stable URL and collision behavior** — Trust model: user. Blast radius: fallback ports break bookmark/static URL; hard failure blocks dashboard until fixed. Alternatives: fixed 8765 fail loudly, fallback port, reuse healthy dashboard if detectable. User resolved: use `http://127.0.0.1:8765/`; fail loudly on collision.
4. **Auth source and setup prompt** — Trust model: agent. Blast radius: env-only token creates false degraded state; noisy auth failures undermine dashboard. Alternatives: `GITHUB_TOKEN` only, `gh auth token` fallback, or manual token file. Recommendation: `GITHUB_TOKEN` first, then `gh auth token`; show inline setup card if unavailable.
5. **Low API strategy** — Trust model: agent. Blast radius: background service may create API churn or CPU load. Alternatives: per-request live reads, shared long-lived client, TTL/in-flight de-dupe, manual refresh only. Recommendation: shared state builder/client with TTL and in-flight de-dupe bounded by refresh interval.
6. **Config ownership** — Trust model: agent + user. Blast radius: repo-local config mutates observed repos unnecessarily; user-level config may be harder to discover. Alternatives: require `--config`, repo-local config, user-level generated config. Recommendation: user-level generated default config for the two PokeEdge repos, with docs and CLI override.

### Draft Spec Deltas

Likely new capability/spec: `local-dashboard` or an `advance-meta` addition if local dashboard remains CLI-adjacent.

- `rq-localDashboardAutostart01`: Given dashboard autostart is installed and the user manager starts, when the machine boots or the user session is created, then the dashboard service runs in the background without opening a browser.
- `rq-localDashboardStableUrl01`: Given the dashboard service starts, when port 8765 is available, then the dashboard is reachable at `http://127.0.0.1:8765/`; when unavailable, startup fails with a clear diagnostic and no silent fallback port.
- `rq-localDashboardPokeedgeConfig01`: Given default dashboard config is generated, when the dashboard reads it, then it includes `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge` and `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge-web` with the correct GitHub repositories.
- `rq-localDashboardAuthPrompt01`: Given GitHub authentication is unavailable, when the dashboard renders, then it shows an inline setup prompt and continues to show available local/ADV state without leaking secrets.
- `rq-localDashboardLowApi01`: Given browser refreshes or multiple tabs call `/api/state`, when requests occur within the configured refresh interval, then GitHub reads are cached or coalesced so repeated requests do not create repeated full GitHub fanout.
- `rq-localDashboardReadOnly01`: Given the autostarted dashboard is open, when the user interacts with it, then no ADV, GitHub, deployment, repository, or service mutation control is available from the browser UI.

### Related Pattern Scan

- Similar CLI pattern: `bin/adv` already owns read-only local commands (`status`, `roadmap`, `slop-scan`, `dashboard`) and should remain the entrypoint.
- Similar config parser: `bin/lib/dashboard/config.ts` already validates schema and absolute paths; can be extended with default config loading/generation rather than replaced.
- Similar service precedent: `~/.config/systemd/user/temporal-dev.service` and `vision.service` provide local examples for `Type=simple`, `Restart=on-failure`, `WantedBy=default.target`, journal logging, and PATH environment handling.
- Similar performance warning: `repo-improve-prep` documents default status read doing too much work; dashboard must avoid a new hot-path all-sources recomputation pattern.
- No existing `adv dashboard` autostart config/service found in `~/.config/systemd/user` or autostart scan.

### Completeness Verification

- Problem-completeness: high confidence. The problem is not just `--config` friction; it includes lifecycle availability, stable URL, specific PokeEdge config, auth setup clarity, and low API/CPU behavior.
- Solution-scope: high confidence for discovery. Intended scope includes service lifecycle, lingering, default config, auth fallback/setup prompt, URL collision behavior, and API caching/de-dupe. It explicitly excludes hosted service, write console, BI/trends, auto-open browser, and provider-specific deployment expansion.
- Sole-entry claim status: no unverified sole-entry claim is required. Discovery identifies multiple affected surfaces: CLI entrypoint, dashboard config, dashboard server/state builder, GitHub token/client, docs, and user service installer/config.
- Secondary-surface dispositions: user service setup is in scope; repo-local PokeEdge code changes are out of scope unless discovery/design proves repo-local config is necessary; desktop autostart/XDG is out of scope as a rejected alternative; dashboard UI auth prompt is in scope; write controls remain out of scope.
- Reproduction finding classification: no failed-operation reproduction is being turned into a criterion. Existing manual-start behavior is an approved new capability gap, not a defect in an already-promised autostart capability.

### LBP Check

- User systemd service is the long-term boring fit for Linux login/background lifecycle in this environment: systemd service units supervise long-running processes, `Type=simple` assumes the process continues, and user services use `WantedBy=default.target`. Existing local services already follow this pattern.
- XDG autostart is viable for graphical session startup but is less fitting because the user wants background service availability even logged out; freedesktop autostart is desktop-session oriented and may be generator-managed by desktop environments.
- Shell hooks are rejected because they depend on interactive shells and do not provide stable supervision or logout behavior.
- OpenCode wrapper startup is rejected because the user wants login-level availability independent of opening OpenCode.
- `gh auth token` fallback is LBP for local GitHub auth because GitHub CLI is installed and authenticated locally; env-only `GITHUB_TOKEN` is too brittle.
- Shared client/cache with TTL/in-flight de-dupe is LBP for low API use because the current implementation creates a fresh GitHub client per request and otherwise loses ETag cache value.
- Fresh external research found no need for a hosted dashboard framework; the problem is local lifecycle/config/auth, not a product-dashboard stack.

### Discovery Opportunity Scout

- Trigger decision: run — strategic lifecycle/config/performance surface with external platform choice.
- Candidates considered: 5.
- Auto-adopted/design-adopted: 4.
  - Reuse GitHub client/cache + TTL/in-flight de-dupe.
  - Add `gh auth token` fallback and setup prompt.
  - Use user systemd service with `default.target` and lingering behavior.
  - Generate/provide PokeEdge default config.
- Surfaced to user: fixed URL/collision behavior; user chose 8765 and fail loudly.
- Scout validation: caution only; cache-reuse must preserve per-source degradation isolation.

### AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS

Coverage: B:C F:C S:C M:C

No blocking ambiguity findings.

Advisory resolved during discovery:
- S1 ADVISORY Completion Signals: proposal said `stable local URL`; user resolved to `http://127.0.0.1:8765/` and fail loudly on collision.
- M1 ADVISORY Missing Information: proposal said `GitHub setup guidance`; user resolved to inline setup card and local token persistence expectation.
- M2 ADVISORY Missing Information: proposal said `run in the background after login`; user resolved to always running even logged out, requiring lingering/boot-capable behavior.

### Recommended Objectives

1. Make `adv dashboard` available as an always-running local user service for the PokeEdge dashboard.
2. Serve the dashboard at stable URL `http://127.0.0.1:8765/` and fail clearly on port collision.
3. Provide or generate default config for `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge` and `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge-web`.
4. Keep the dashboard local-only and read-only.
5. Keep CPU/API overhead low through bounded refresh, cache reuse, and request coalescing.
6. Use local GitHub auth from `GITHUB_TOKEN` or `gh auth token`; show inline setup guidance when unavailable.
7. Document installation, service health, logs, lingering behavior, and troubleshooting.

### User Decisions

- Stable URL: use `http://127.0.0.1:8765/`.
- Port collision: fail loudly; do not silently use fallback port.
- GitHub auth unavailable: show inline setup card.
- Service lifetime: should always run, including when logged out; discovery evidence shows `Linger=no`, so setup must handle or clearly guide lingering.

### Agent Decisions

- Prefer user systemd service over XDG autostart, shell hooks, or OpenCode wrapper.
- Prefer user-level generated config over repo-local config in observed PokeEdge repos unless design proves otherwise.
- Prefer `GITHUB_TOKEN` plus `gh auth token` fallback over env-only auth.
- Prefer server-lifetime caching/coalescing over per-request fresh clients.

# Agreement

## Objectives

1. Make the local dashboard available as an always-running local user service for PokeEdge work.
2. Serve the dashboard at stable URL `http://127.0.0.1:8765/` and fail clearly on port collision.
3. Provide default dashboard configuration for `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge` and `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge-web`.
4. Keep the dashboard local-only and read-only.
5. Keep CPU/API overhead low through bounded refresh, cache reuse, and request coalescing.
6. Use local GitHub authentication from `GITHUB_TOKEN` or `gh auth token`; show inline setup guidance when unavailable.
7. Document installation, service health, logs, lingering behavior, and troubleshooting.

## Success Criteria

- SC1: User can open `http://127.0.0.1:8765/` after login or reboot without manually running `bin/adv dashboard`.
- SC2: User can see both `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge` and `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge-web` from one local dashboard view.
- SC3: Dashboard remains local-only and read-only while running as a background service.
- SC4: Dashboard avoids noticeable background load by not repeating full GitHub/API fetch work more often than the configured refresh cadence.
- SC5: When GitHub auth is unavailable, user sees clear inline setup guidance instead of only a noisy degraded state.

## Acceptance Criteria

- AC1: Given dashboard autostart is installed, when the user manager starts after boot/login, then the dashboard service starts in the background and serves `GET /` at `http://127.0.0.1:8765/` without opening a browser.
- AC2: Given the user is logged out after setup, when the user service manager is active via lingering, then the dashboard service remains configured to run; if lingering is unavailable or disabled, setup reports the exact status and remediation.
- AC3: Given default dashboard config is used, when `/api/state` is requested, then the response includes projects for `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge` and `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge-web` with GitHub repos `Sharper-Flow/PokeEdge` and `Sharper-Flow/PokeEdge-Web`.
- AC4: Given port `8765` is occupied by a non-dashboard process, when the service starts, then startup fails with a clear diagnostic and does not silently choose another port.
- AC5: Given `GITHUB_TOKEN` is unset but `gh auth token` succeeds, when the dashboard reads GitHub state, then GitHub data is not degraded solely because `GITHUB_TOKEN` is absent.
- AC6: Given neither `GITHUB_TOKEN` nor `gh auth token` is available, when the dashboard renders, then it shows an inline GitHub setup card with local remediation guidance and does not expose tokens or secrets.
- AC7: Given repeated `/api/state` requests occur within the configured refresh interval, when GitHub reads are needed, then requests are cached or coalesced so one browser/tab burst does not trigger repeated full GitHub fanout per project.
- AC8: Given one configured project is missing, not ADV-enabled, or temporarily unreadable, when the dashboard loads, then that project/source is shown as degraded and the other configured project still renders.
- AC9: Given the dashboard is open, when the user interacts with it or sends non-GET requests, then no ADV, GitHub, deployment, repository, or service mutation is available, and unsupported methods are rejected.

## Constraints

- C1: The dashboard must remain local-only.
- C2: The dashboard browser UI and API must remain read-only.
- C3: The default stable URL is `http://127.0.0.1:8765/`.
- C4: Port collision must fail clearly; no silent fallback port.
- C5: The service must not open a browser automatically.
- C6: Background behavior must prioritize low CPU/API usage over aggressive refresh.
- C7: Config/logs/browser output must not expose GitHub tokens or auth material.
- C8: The implementation must preserve source-specific degradation; one source/project failure must not blank the whole dashboard.

## Avoidances

- DONT1: Do not create a hosted dashboard service.
- DONT2: Do not add write actions or mutation controls for ADV, GitHub, deployment, repository, or service state.
- DONT3: Do not turn the dashboard into generic BI, trend analytics, or historical reporting.
- DONT4: Do not auto-open a browser tab at login.
- DONT5: Do not expand into provider-specific deployment API integrations.
- DONT6: Do not store or display GitHub tokens or secrets.
- DONT7: Do not use high-frequency polling that creates noticeable CPU load or API churn.

## Out of Scope

- OOS1: Hosted multi-user dashboard or external backend.
- OOS2: Dashboard write/mutation controls.
- OOS3: BI/trend analytics, historical reporting, or portfolio reporting.
- OOS4: Auto-opening a browser tab at login.
- OOS5: Provider-specific deployment API expansion.
- OOS6: Repo-local PokeEdge code changes unless design proves repo-local config is necessary.

## Preview Applicability

visual_surface: true

Rationale: The change affects a browser-visible dashboard and GitHub-auth-unavailable UI state. Review must verify preview reachability, stable URL behavior, inline setup prompt behavior, and no mutation controls.

## Decisions

### User Decisions

- Stable URL: use `http://127.0.0.1:8765/`.
- Port collision behavior: fail loudly; do not silently choose a fallback port.
- GitHub auth unavailable behavior: show an inline setup card.
- Service lifetime: should always run even when logged out; setup must handle or guide lingering/boot-capable user-service behavior.
- Browser behavior: background-only; do not open browser automatically.
- Scope avoidances: no hosted service, no write console, no generic BI/trends dashboard.

### Agent Decisions (LBP)

- Prefer a user systemd service over XDG autostart, shell hooks, or OpenCode wrapper because current environment supports `systemctl --user`, existing local services use it, and it provides supervision/journal logs.
- Include lingering detection/remediation because current `loginctl show-user $USER --property=Linger` returned `Linger=no` and the user wants service availability after logout.
- Prefer user-level generated/default config for PokeEdge projects over mutating the observed repos.
- Prefer `GITHUB_TOKEN` first, then `gh auth token` fallback, because `gh` is installed/authenticated locally and env-only auth creates false degradation.
- Prefer server-lifetime GitHub client/cache plus TTL/in-flight de-dupe over per-request fresh clients.

## Deferred Questions

None.

## Sign-Off

Acceptance criteria and success criteria approved by user reply `approve` on 2026-06-26 during `/adv-discover improveDashboardAutostart`.

# Design

## Architecture Overview

Add a lightweight dashboard autostart layer around the existing `bin/adv dashboard` runtime.

The design keeps the browser dashboard read-only and local-only. The only setup mutations happen through an explicit CLI install/doctor surface that writes user-level config and a user systemd service. Runtime dashboard requests continue to expose only `GET /` and `GET /api/state`.

Core pieces:

1. **User-level dashboard profile** — generated config for the PokeEdge pair under a user config directory.
2. **User systemd service** — starts the dashboard on the stable loopback URL and keeps it supervised.
3. **Linger handling** — setup detects/enables or reports lingering so the service can run after logout.
4. **Low-API runtime cache** — server-lifetime GitHub client/cache plus TTL and in-flight request coalescing.
5. **Local auth fallback** — `GITHUB_TOKEN` first, then bounded `gh auth token`.
6. **Inline setup card** — GitHub-auth-unavailable state is rendered as clear setup guidance, not just noisy degradation.

## Key Decisions

### D1 — User systemd service is the autostart mechanism

Use a user systemd service named `adv-dashboard-pokeedge.service` installed under `~/.config/systemd/user/`.

Rationale:
- Current machine has working `systemctl --user`.
- Existing local services (`temporal-dev.service`, `vision.service`) use this model.
- `WantedBy=default.target` is the canonical user-service enable target.
- systemd gives supervision, restart policy, journal logs, and explicit health/status checks.

Rejected alternatives:
- XDG autostart: desktop-session oriented and weaker for logged-out/lingering behavior.
- Shell profile hook: depends on interactive shells and lacks supervision.
- OpenCode wrapper hook: does not satisfy login/boot availability independent of OpenCode.

### D2 — Stable URL is fixed at `http://127.0.0.1:8765/`

The service binds to `127.0.0.1:8765`. Port collision is a hard startup failure.

Design requirements:
- Bun server startup uses explicit non-reuse behavior where supported (`reusePort: false`) and wraps `Bun.serve` in try/catch.
- Port collision exits with a distinct non-restart exit status.
- The systemd unit uses `RestartPreventExitStatus` for that exit status and `StartLimitIntervalSec` / `StartLimitBurst` as a restart-storm backstop.
- No silent fallback port.

### D3 — Installer writes user-level PokeEdge config

Add an explicit install/profile command, e.g. `bin/adv dashboard install --profile pokeedge`, that writes default config to a user-owned config path such as:

```text
~/.config/advance/dashboard/pokeedge.json
```

Generated config:

```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "refresh_seconds": 45,
  "projects": [
    {
      "id": "pokeedge",
      "label": "PokeEdge",
      "path": "/home/jon/dev/pokeedge",
      "github": { "owner": "Sharper-Flow", "repo": "PokeEdge" }
    },
    {
      "id": "pokeedge-web",
      "label": "PokeEdge Web",
      "path": "/home/jon/dev/pokeedge-web",
      "github": { "owner": "Sharper-Flow", "repo": "PokeEdge-Web" }
    }
  ]
}
```

Rationale:
- Observed repos are inputs; no repo-local PokeEdge code mutation is needed.
- User-level config matches user-specific machine paths and auth context.
- Existing config parser already validates absolute paths and repo refs.

### D4 — Installer/doctor owns service setup and linger visibility

Add a dashboard doctor/status command, e.g. `bin/adv dashboard doctor --profile pokeedge`, to report:

- config path exists and parses
- service unit exists
- `systemctl --user is-enabled` / `is-active` status
- `loginctl show-user $USER --property=Linger`
- URL reachability for `GET /` and `/api/state`
- GitHub auth source status without printing tokens

For the install command:
- writes config and service unit
- runs `systemctl --user daemon-reload`
- runs `systemctl --user enable --now adv-dashboard-pokeedge.service`
- attempts caller-scoped `loginctl enable-linger` when needed, or reports exact remediation if permission/platform blocks it

This satisfies the user expectation that the dashboard should run even after logout while keeping setup explicit and auditable.

### D5 — Runtime cache/coalescing protects CPU/API budget

Refactor dashboard state serving so `createDashboardHandler` can own a long-lived state provider rather than rebuilding all source clients per request.

Behavior:
- Shared GitHub dashboard client lives for the server process lifetime.
- `/api/state` uses a TTL equal to configured `refresh_seconds`.
- Concurrent requests within a refresh window share one in-flight state build.
- Cached state is returned for repeated requests within TTL.
- Per-source degradation semantics are preserved; a failed project/source does not poison the whole cache beyond the normal refresh TTL.

Rationale:
- Current `buildDashboardState` creates a fresh GitHub client on each request, discarding ETag cache.
- Multiple browser tabs should not create repeated full GitHub fanout.

### D6 — GitHub auth uses env first, then `gh auth token`

Token provider order:

1. `GITHUB_TOKEN` if non-empty.
2. Bounded `gh auth token` subprocess.
3. Auth-unavailable degraded source with setup metadata.

Rules:
- Token values are never logged, returned, persisted, or rendered.
- Subprocess stderr/stdout is not surfaced raw.
- `gh auth token` has a short timeout and does not block the whole dashboard indefinitely.
- If auth is unavailable, local/ADV state still renders.

### D7 — Auth-unavailable state renders as setup card

For degraded source `github/GITHUB_AUTH_UNAVAILABLE`, UI renders a concise setup card:

- explains GitHub auth is needed for PR/Actions/deployment data
- suggests local remediation, e.g. `gh auth login` or setting `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- states dashboard remains read-only
- does not show token values or raw auth output

## ADR Drafts

None. Decisions are important but not hard-to-reverse enough to require ADRs; systemd service naming and config paths can be revised in follow-up if needed.

## Implementation Strategy

1. **Profile constants and config generation**
   - Add PokeEdge profile definition with two projects and default port/config path/service name.
   - Add tests that generated config parses and uses correct repos/paths.

2. **Dashboard CLI subcommands**
   - Extend `bin/adv dashboard` parsing to support install/doctor/status-like actions while preserving existing `dashboard --config` server mode.
   - Keep `--config`, `--host`, and `--port` for manual use.
   - Ensure `--help` documents profile install and doctor commands.

3. **Systemd unit generation**
   - Generate deterministic unit content with `Type=simple`, `WantedBy=default.target`, journal output, fixed URL args, PATH environment, `Restart=on-failure`, `RestartPreventExitStatus=<port-collision-code>`, and start-limit settings.
   - Add unit-content tests; avoid relying on host systemd in unit tests.

4. **Install/doctor runtime operations**
   - Add process adapter seams for `systemctl`, `loginctl`, and URL reachability checks so behavior is testable without mutating host state.
   - Install command writes files, reloads/enables/starts service, checks/attempts lingering, then prints stable URL and diagnostics.
   - Doctor command reports status without mutation unless an explicit repair/install path is invoked.

5. **Port collision hardening**
   - Wrap `Bun.serve` startup with typed error classification.
   - Disable port reuse where supported.
   - Return distinct collision exit code so systemd does not restart-loop on occupied 8765.
   - Add regression that pre-binds the port and asserts clear failure/no fallback.

6. **State cache/coalescing**
   - Add server-lifetime state provider with TTL and in-flight promise de-dupe.
   - Keep existing `buildDashboardState` usable for pure tests.
   - Add tests proving multiple requests inside TTL do not call GitHub reader repeatedly.

7. **GitHub auth fallback and setup card**
   - Extend token provider with bounded `gh auth token` fallback.
   - Add structured auth-degraded remediation data.
   - Update UI to render setup card for auth-unavailable source.
   - Add tests for env token, gh fallback, no-auth setup card, and token redaction.

8. **Docs**
   - Document `adv dashboard install --profile pokeedge`, stable URL, service status/log commands, lingering behavior, disable/uninstall path, and troubleshooting.

## LBP Analysis

This approach is the lowest-boring-path for the agreed constraints:

- It reuses the existing dashboard CLI/server instead of introducing a hosted service or frontend stack.
- User systemd services are already present in the local environment and provide reliable supervision with logs.
- User-level config avoids mutating the PokeEdge repos while still making the dashboard specific to this machine.
- Caching/coalescing addresses the actual low-API risk in the current implementation: fresh clients and repeated fanout per `/api/state` call.
- `gh auth token` fallback matches the operator's existing GitHub CLI auth and prevents false degradation when `GITHUB_TOKEN` is unset.

## Affected Components

- `bin/adv`
  - dashboard subcommand parsing
  - install/doctor actions
  - server startup error handling

- `bin/lib/dashboard/config.ts`
  - profile/default config helpers
  - maybe config path resolution helpers

- `bin/lib/dashboard/server.ts`
  - reusable state provider / cache / coalescing
  - server startup options and collision handling

- `bin/lib/dashboard/github.ts`
  - token provider fallback and auth-unavailable remediation metadata

- `bin/lib/dashboard/ui.ts`
  - inline GitHub setup card

- `bin/dashboard-cli.test.ts` and `bin/lib/dashboard/*.test.ts`
  - CLI install/doctor behavior
  - systemd unit generation
  - port collision
  - caching/coalescing
  - auth fallback/setup prompt

- `README.md` or dashboard docs
  - install/run/status/logs/uninstall/linger guidance

## Design-Derived Criteria

- DDC1: `/api/state` cache TTL must be tied to `refresh_seconds`; repeated requests within the TTL must not trigger repeated full GitHub reads per project.
- DDC2: Concurrent `/api/state` requests while refresh is in progress must share one in-flight refresh.
- DDC3: `gh auth token` fallback must be bounded by a short timeout and must not surface raw stdout/stderr in dashboard output.
- DDC4: Port collision on `127.0.0.1:8765` must produce a distinct exit code used by the generated systemd unit's `RestartPreventExitStatus`.
- DDC5: Generated service unit must include restart-storm limits.
- DDC6: Install/doctor commands must be testable through fake process/filesystem/network adapters; unit tests must not mutate real `~/.config/systemd/user`.
- DDC7: All auth/setup diagnostics must be token-redacted through existing dashboard sanitizer or stricter equivalent.

## Risks / Mitigations

- Risk: enabling lingering may need privileges or fail on non-systemd systems.
  - Mitigation: installer/doctor reports exact status and remediation; design keeps service unit user-scoped.

- Risk: systemd-specific design is Linux-specific.
  - Mitigation: current environment is Linux/systemd; design keeps manual `--config` server mode intact for other environments.

- Risk: caching can hide a transient recovery for up to one refresh interval.
  - Mitigation: TTL is bounded by existing 30–60s refresh semantics; failures remain visible as degraded and retry on next refresh.

- Risk: `gh auth token` subprocess may hang or output sensitive data.
  - Mitigation: short timeout, no raw output rendering, token sanitizer, tests for redaction.

- Risk: service restart loop on port collision.
  - Mitigation: distinct exit code, `RestartPreventExitStatus`, and `StartLimit*` settings.

## Design Leverage Scout

- Candidates considered: 5.
- Auto-adopted: 4.
  - Structural port-collision failure via explicit no-reuse/startup catch/distinct exit code.
  - Server-lifetime GitHub client/cache with TTL and in-flight de-dupe.
  - Restart-storm backstop through systemd start limits.
  - Linger handling in install/doctor flow.
- Surfaced to user: none after routing; exit code value and exact linger operation are technical implementation details within approved behavior.
- Validator note for later review: port collision behavior must be TDD-proven because current `Bun.serve` call lacks explicit handling.

## Validation Result

Validator: VALIDATED.

Findings:
- Correctness: design maps to AC1-AC9. User systemd service plus lingering covers lifecycle; fixed port plus port-collision handling covers stable URL; cache/coalescing covers low API; auth fallback/setup card covers GitHub setup.
- Simplicity: no materially simpler approach satisfies always-running-after-logout plus supervision/logging; rejected alternatives are correctly out of scope.
- Spec-law: no existing spec conflict found for dashboard/autostart surface.
- Alternatives: XDG autostart, shell hooks, OpenCode wrapper, and hosted dashboard alternatives were considered; none better fit the agreement.

Non-blocking planning notes:
- Reserve a disjoint port-collision exit code and wire it consistently to Bun startup and `RestartPreventExitStatus`.
- Consider `Type=exec` instead of `Type=simple` if implementation can preserve compatibility and improve start-failure reporting.
- Unit-test linger-denied path through fake process adapters.

# Executive Summary

## Outcome

Implemented PokeEdge dashboard autostart support in Advance: a local-only read-only dashboard profile, install/doctor commands, restart-safe user systemd unit generation, GitHub auth fallback/setup guidance, and low-churn `/api/state` caching/coalescing.

## Verdict

APPROVED

## What Was Built

1. Added PokeEdge dashboard profile/default config helpers for `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge` and `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge-web` with `Sharper-Flow/PokeEdge` and `Sharper-Flow/PokeEdge-Web`.
2. Hardened dashboard startup so occupied fixed port `8765` fails loudly with exit code `75` and no silent fallback.
3. Added server-lifetime `/api/state` cache/coalescing keyed to configured `refresh_seconds`.
4. Added `GITHUB_TOKEN`-first, bounded `gh auth token` fallback plus safe auth-unavailable setup metadata.
5. Added inline GitHub setup-card UI while preserving read-only/no-mutation behavior.
6. Added `adv dashboard install --profile pokeedge` and `adv dashboard doctor --profile pokeedge`.
7. Added restart-safe user systemd unit generation with `WantedBy=default.target`, `RestartPreventExitStatus=75`, journal logging, and linger diagnostics/remediation.
8. Documented install, stable URL, health/log commands, lingering, uninstall, fixed-port behavior, GitHub auth, and local-only/read-only constraints.

## What Was Verified

- Verdict: APPROVED with 0 blockers/issues; independent reviewer report `improveDashboardAutostart|change:review:acceptance|adv-reviewer|1` returned READY.
- Tests: `bun test bin/` passed in run `tr_mqu9uvzi_93294154` — 149 tests across 23 files, 417 expects, 0 failures.
- Preview URL: local loopback preview verified in run `tr_mqua4gap_96126434`: `http://127.0.0.1:43372/` returned `GET /` 200 and `GET /api/state` 200 during verification. Stable installed URL is `http://127.0.0.1:8765/`.
- Contract matrix: 35/35 required rows passed/respected; 0 failed/violated/unknown.

## Remaining Concerns

None for the accepted contract. Operator still must run `bin/adv dashboard install --profile pokeedge` to enable the real user service.
